description: If something is P, and all things that are P make B true, then B is true.

hints.text: You're given two premises: there exists something that is $P$, and everything that is $P$ implies $B$. The conclusion is $B$. The strategy: open the existential witness, use the universal to get $P(witness) \to B$, then close. Assume both premises to start.
Assume the existential: there is some $P$.
Assume the universal: everything $P$ leads to $B$.
Instantiate the universal with the witness term $y$ using `forall-elim`.
Merge $y$ with $P(y)$ using `and-intro` to bring them into one context.
Apply modus ponens: $P(y) \to B$ (step 5) and $P(y)$ (step 7) yield $B$.
Close the witness with `exists-elim` — the existential (step 1), the witness subproof (step 8), and the witness variable $y$. The engine discharges both the witness atom and its instantiated body.
